methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Realta Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $36M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, among them Fuse Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo. However, a reliable peer-median multiple could not be derived; the private comp universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints with comparable valuation tiers to support triangulation via that method.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable approach here. Using total capital raised ($36M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $72M. This reflects the conventional expectation that a Series A round represents approximately half the pre-money valuation at Series B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $61M (low) to $83M (high), with a median of $72M. This range assumes typical step-up dynamics hold for frontier nuclear technologies at this stage. Key uncertainties include: the absence of granular peer multiples for direct calibration; variation in burn rates and time-to-next-milestone across the nuclear sector; and regulatory and financing-pathway risks inherent to the sector that may compress or expand investor step-up expectations. The single-method triangulation underscores the need for updated peer data as the market develops.
